answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: Convert the given time into total minutes. Add the extra minutes, then take modulo 1440 (minutes in a day). Convert back to HH:MM format using division and modulo.

answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: Convert both times into minutes. If end < start, add 1440 to end time. Then subtract start from end.

answers.author: System Hint
answers.content: Handle next-day case using: if end < start → end += 1440

answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: Map days to indices (0–6). Add n and take modulo 7. Convert index back to day name.

answers.author: System Hint
answers.content: days = [Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri, Sat, Sun]; result = days[(index + n) % 7]

answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: Convert both dates into total days from a base date. Account for leap years. Then return absolute difference.

answers.author: System Hint
answers.content: Leap year rule: year % 4 == 0 AND (year % 100 != 0 OR year % 400 == 0)

answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: Convert both intervals into minutes. Find overlap using max(start1, start2) and min(end1, end2). If overlap exists, subtract.

answers.author: System Hint
answers.content: Overlap = max(0, min(end1, end2) - max(start1, start2))
